message:
perf(record): replace resize+copy+truncate with reserve+extend append

encode_record_with_header_template was doing:
  buf.resize(total_capacity, 0);        // zero-fill max_body_size bytes
  buf[..header_size].copy_from_slice(&header.template);
  // ... slot loop writing encoded values into [body_offset..end] ...
  buf.truncate(body_offset);            // shrink back to actual length

The zero-fill was wasted work for every row — we immediately overwrote
the prefix with the template and then overwrote each slot with the
encoded value, while the tail past body_offset was always truncated.
On wide rows with many NULLs or small integers the zero-fill dominated
the encode time.

New shape:
  buf.reserve(total_capacity);                // just capacity hint
  buf.extend_from_slice(&header.template);    // header goes in
  // ... slot loop calls append_serialized_value which extends buf ...
  // no truncate needed — buf.len() is already exactly body_offset

Introduced helper `append_serialized_value` parallel to the existing
`encode_serialized_value` but using buf.extend_from_slice so the caller
doesn't need to track body_offset. A debug_assert at the tail of each
append verifies the per-slot byte count matches payload_len, catching
header-template / value mismatch during tests without a release-build
penalty.

Behaviour identical; removes one O(total_capacity) memset per row.
